questions.question: What is a ratio?
questions.correct_answer: A comparison of two quantities using division
questions.explanation: Ratios compare quantities and are written in the form a:b or a/b.

questions.question: How is a ratio written?
questions.correct_answer: Using a colon (:) between two quantities
questions.explanation: For example, the ratio of 4 to 5 is written as 4:5.

questions.question: What are equivalent ratios?
questions.correct_answer: Different ratios that represent the same relationship between quantities
questions.explanation: They are obtained by multiplying or dividing both terms of a ratio by the same number.

questions.question: What is a proportion?
questions.correct_answer: An equation that states two ratios are equal

questions.question: What is cross multiplication in proportions?
questions.correct_answer: Multiplying the numerator of one ratio with the denominator of the other ratio
questions.explanation: It helps verify whether two ratios form a proportion.

questions.question: What is a unit rate?
questions.correct_answer: A rate expressed for one unit of a quantity
questions.explanation: For example, if 5 apples cost ₹50, the unit rate is ₹10 per apple.

questions.question: What is direct proportion?
questions.correct_answer: A relationship where two quantities increase or decrease together in the same ratio
questions.explanation: If one quantity doubles, the other also doubles.

questions.question: Give an example of proportional reasoning in daily life.
questions.correct_answer: Scaling a recipe for more people
questions.explanation: Ingredients are increased in the same ratio when serving more people.

questions.question: How can we simplify a ratio?
questions.correct_answer: By dividing both terms of the ratio by their HCF
questions.explanation: This converts the ratio into its simplest form.

questions.question: What is the ratio of 8 to 12 in simplest form?
questions.explanation: Both numbers are divided by their HCF which is 4.

notes.title: Ratio
notes.content: A ratio compares two quantities and is written in the form a:b.

notes.title: Equivalent Ratios
notes.content: Equivalent ratios represent the same relationship and are obtained by multiplying or dividing both terms by the same number.

notes.title: Proportion
notes.content: A proportion states that two ratios are equal.

notes.title: Direct Proportion
notes.content: Two quantities are directly proportional if they increase or decrease in the same ratio.

notes.title: Unit Rate
notes.content: A unit rate shows the value of one quantity for a single unit of another quantity.

facts.fact: Ratios compare quantities of the same kind.
Equivalent ratios represent the same relationship between numbers.
In a proportion, cross products of the ratios are equal.
Direct proportion is commonly used in real-life situations such as speed, cost and recipes.
